_meta.name: RunPod Operational Knowledge (ossified)
_meta.description: RunPod H100 pod provisioning, SSH, training, troubleshooting, cost, and deployment — permanent reference for anima project.
related_ssot: shared/config/runpod.json — live pod state + standard recipe + GPU catalog
shared/config/secrets_registry.json — R2/API key location registry (no actual keys)
training/CLAUDE.md — training rules, MFS quota, R2 checkpoint policy

graphql_examples.list_pods_curl: curl -s -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Authorization: Bearer $RUNPOD_API_KEY' -d '{"query":"{ myself { pods { id name runtime { gpus { id gpuUtilPercent memoryUtilPercent } uptimeInSeconds ports { ip isIpPublic privatePort publicPort type } } desiredStatus imageName gpuCount costPerHr } } }"}' https://api.runpod.io/graphql
graphql_examples.note: runpodctl wraps GraphQL — prefer CLI over raw curl for standard ops

connection_pattern.step_1: runpodctl ssh info <POD_ID>  # returns JSON with ip + port (port changes every restart!)

no_route_to_host.symptom: ssh: connect to host ... port ...: No route to host
no_route_to_host.cause: Wrong IP or port — pod may have restarted and port changed
no_route_to_host.fix: Re-run 'runpodctl ssh info <id>' to get current IP:PORT

proxy_url_does_not_work.cause: RunPod proxy SSH format unreliable for anima usage
proxy_url_does_not_work.fix: Always use 'runpodctl ssh info' to get direct IP:PORT

semicolon_chain_fails.cause: Multiple commands chained with ; fail over SSH
semicolon_chain_fails.fix: Use 'bash -c "cmd1 && cmd2"' or separate SSH calls

tmux_new_session_silent_fail.symptom: tmux new-session command returns but session not created
tmux_new_session_silent_fail.fix: Use 'bash -c "nohup python -u ... > log 2>&1 &"' or split SSH commands

H100_80GB_HBM3.use: Primary training GPU — all anima training runs

H100_NVL.use: Alternative H100 variant — not yet tested

A100_80GB.use: DECOMMISSIONED for anima — inference/runtime only, training forbidden

pods.custom_image: dancindocker/anima:v2 (prebuilt, for rapid redeployment from R2)

rust_paths.note: Installed under /opt/ to avoid RunPod overlay FS conflict with /root/.rustup

typical_volumes.note: No persistent volume — /workspace is container-local MooseFS. All important data must be R2-backed.

pods.apt_dependencies: apt-get update -qq && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq build-essential clang lld git curl ca-certificates libblis-dev

hexa_binaries_on_pod./usr/local/bin/hexa: Rust interpreter (default, used by most training runs)
hexa_binaries_on_pod.switch_convention: Current in-flight training uses whichever was started. Next round chooses explicitly.

priority_order.use_for: Inference, serving, small model training (<= 14B inference)
priority_order.rule: Always try Ubuntu first for any GPU task

priority_order.use_for: 32B+ training, 72B inference, anything Ubuntu cannot handle
priority_order.rule: Last resort only — must state why Ubuntu is insufficient

gpu_policy.mac_policy: Mac = orchestration/code editing ONLY. GPU training absolutely forbidden on Mac.

auto_launch_authorization.condition: When pursuing AGI goal (CLM + ALM dual track)
auto_launch_authorization.rule: Complete training -> immediately stop+remove pod (zero idle cost)

h100_free_use.rule: H100 pods freely used within 2-pod limit. Never stop the training loop due to pod availability.
h100_free_use.fallback: If pods unavailable (out of stock, booting), continue non-training work in parallel. Launch training immediately when pod becomes available.

gpu_policy.training_gpu_restriction: H100 only for training. A100/4090 = runtime/inference only. No exceptions.
gpu_policy.quantization_ban: 4-bit, 8-bit, GPTQ, AWQ, bnb quantization absolutely forbidden — performance degradation unacceptable.

mfs_quota.description: /workspace on RunPod uses MooseFS with per-session quota. df may show TB free, but checkpoint saves can silently fail.

symptoms.torch_save_silent_exit: Python process exits during torch.save with no error message
symptoms.partial_checkpoint: Checkpoint file exists but truncated (0.5-6 GB instead of expected 18 GB)
symptoms.no_dmesg_kill_log: dmesg shows no OOM kill — MFS quota enforcement is silent
symptoms.cuda_malloc_cascade: After quota hit, subsequent CUDA allocations fail with error 700

preflight_check.command: dd if=/dev/zero of=/workspace/_q_test bs=1M count=20000 status=none && rm /workspace/_q_test
preflight_check.purpose: Write 20GB test file to verify MFS quota has headroom. If dd fails, rm -rf old data and retry.

mandatory_rules.pre_training: rm -rf /workspace/ckpt_* (delete ALL previous checkpoint directories before starting new training)
mandatory_rules.during_training: NO in-Python R2 upload. Record DEFERRED upload queue only. No synchronous rclone after torch.save.
mandatory_rules.post_training: rclone copy entire checkpoint directory to R2 in one batch

sync_rclone_after_save.description: torch.save immediately followed by synchronous rclone copy
sync_rclone_after_save.incident: r3 step 2000 crash — rclone + torch.save exceeded session quota

emergency_extra_save.description: phi_emergency_ckpt doing additional torch.save during training
emergency_extra_save.incident: r3b step 4000 crash — extra checkpoint save pushed over quota

manual_pkill_rclone.description: Killing rclone process during training to free bandwidth
manual_pkill_rclone.incident: r3b secondary crash — race condition between rclone and torch.save

buckets.anima-models: Model checkpoints (primary — all training outputs go here)

credentials.rule: NEVER store actual keys in any repo file. Path references only.

naming_convention.rule: No dates in filename — R2 metadata records timestamps automatically. model+round+step is unique.

when_to_upload.rule: AFTER training completes or early-stops. Never during training (MFS quota trap).
when_to_upload.trigger: Training process exit detected (success or early-stop)
when_to_upload.sequence: 1. Training ends -> 2. rclone copy to R2 -> 3. Verify upload -> 4. Delete pod

cuda_cpu_tensor_mismatch.symptom: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device
cuda_cpu_tensor_mismatch.cause: C engine (CPU) passes tensor to bridge/decoder (CUDA) without .to(device)
cuda_cpu_tensor_mismatch.fix: Explicitly move bridge+decoder to device: trinity.bridge = trinity.bridge.to(device); trinity.decoder = trinity.decoder.to(device)

cell_growth_stagnation.symptom: cells=5 stuck, never reaches target cells at step 1200
cell_growth_stagnation.cause: Linear growth formula: target_cells = 4 + step // 500 -> 256 cells needs 126K steps

python_stdout_buffering.symptom: tmux log file empty or not updating during training
python_stdout_buffering.cause: Python buffers stdout by default — tee never receives data
python_stdout_buffering.fix: Always use 'python -u' (unbuffered): tmux new-session -d -s NAME 'python -u train.py ... 2>&1 | tee log.log'
python_stdout_buffering.affected: All Python training scripts on RunPod
python_stdout_buffering.severity: HIGH — invisible training means no monitoring

torch_grad_disabled_globally.symptom: gate.requires_grad = False, no gradients flowing even through bridge
torch_grad_disabled_globally.cause: Benchmark scripts set torch.set_grad_enabled(False) globally, polluting subsequent imports

cuda_error_700_illegal_address.symptom: cuLaunchKernelEx failed: 700 (shmem=0), then cascade of cuda_malloc failures: 700
cuda_error_700_illegal_address.cause: Kernel accesses out-of-bounds memory (array index > buffer size). Once CUDA context is wedged by illegal address, ALL subsequent allocations fail.
cuda_error_700_illegal_address.fix: Fixed with stride-loop pattern in rms_norm + softmax kernels + launch caps for D>1024. Commit 79f5c33b.
cuda_error_700_illegal_address.diagnosis_tool: compute-sanitizer (NVIDIA) to localize the exact kernel. cudaPeekAtLastError() after each launch.
incidents: CLM r4 hexa-native on H100 SM90 — step 3 micro 6 crash (training/deploy/clm_r4_hexa_cuda700_diagnosis.json)
Prior CLM hexa GPU runs on H100 (project_clm_hexa_gpu_plan / project_session_20260412_night)
cuda_error_700_illegal_address.affected: hexa-native CUDA kernels (cuda_kernels.hexa) on H100 with D>1024
cuda_error_700_illegal_address.severity: CRITICAL — blocks entire CLM native GPU training

mfs_quota_silent_crash.symptom: torch.save exits silently, partial checkpoint file, no dmesg kill log
mfs_quota_silent_crash.cause: MooseFS per-session quota exceeded despite df showing TB free
mfs_quota_silent_crash.fix: See mfs_quota section — preflight dd test, delete old ckpts, save_every >= 2000, no in-training rclone
mfs_quota_silent_crash.affected: Any training run that saves large checkpoints (>= 10GB)
mfs_quota_silent_crash.severity: CRITICAL — loses training run with no recovery

peft_torch_incompatibility.cause: peft >= 0.19 references torch.float8_e8m0fnu which was added in torch 2.6. Base image has torch 2.4.1+cu124.
peft_torch_incompatibility.fix: Pin versions: pip install peft==0.13.2 transformers==4.45.2
peft_torch_incompatibility.when_to_unpin: When base image upgrades to torch >= 2.6
peft_torch_incompatibility.affected: Any ALM LoRA training on runpod/pytorch:2.4.0 image

checkpoint_corruption_zero_byte.cause: Training killed (OOM, quota, manual) during torch.save
checkpoint_corruption_zero_byte.fix: Safe save pattern: write to .tmp then os.replace (atomic rename). Cleanup: find /workspace -name '*.pt' -size 0 -delete; find /workspace -name '*.pt.tmp' -delete
checkpoint_corruption_zero_byte.affected: Any training script not using atomic save pattern

gpu_zero_percent_utilization.cause: C engine running entirely on CPU, or small model with instant GPU bursts
gpu_zero_percent_utilization.fix: Check device=cuda in script. For C engines: use larger D models to increase GPU share.

rust_overlay_fs_conflict.symptom: rustup/cargo install fails or loses state after pod restart
rust_overlay_fs_conflict.cause: RunPod overlay filesystem conflicts with /root/.rustup default location
rust_overlay_fs_conflict.fix: Install under /opt/: export RUSTUP_HOME=/opt/rustup CARGO_HOME=/opt/cargo PATH=/opt/cargo/bin:$PATH

scp_wrong_directory.symptom: File uploaded to wrong location (e.g., phi-rs/ root instead of phi-rs/src/)
scp_wrong_directory.cause: scp places file in target directory, not nested subdirectory
scp_wrong_directory.fix: After scp, manually cp to correct location. Example: cp /workspace/phi-rs/lib.rs /workspace/phi-rs/src/lib.rs

omp_duplicate_library.symptom: OMP: Error #15: Initializing libomp.dylib already initialized
omp_duplicate_library.cause: Multiple OpenMP libraries loaded (common on macOS, rare on RunPod)

pod_fleet_variance.symptom: Same software stack gives 31% different step/min across pods
pod_fleet_variance.cause: RunPod H100 SXM pods have heterogeneous hardware performance (binning, cooling, NVLink topology)
pod_fleet_variance.evidence: ubzp169igb7tns: 51.1 step/min vs xhq9b2c8fljdyo: 33.3 step/min (same config, 31.5% gap)
pod_fleet_variance.fix: Tag all MFU measurements with pod_id. Cross-pod comparisons must account for variance. Prefer same-pod A/B tests.

fp8_monkey_patch_broken.cause: transformer_engine.Linear monkey-patch before from_pretrained causes ALL 48 layers to initialize with random weights (HF _extra_state warning)
fp8_monkey_patch_broken.fix: ABANDONED for HF-based LoRA trainer. Correct path requires accelerate.Accelerator(fp8_recipe_handler=...) or full NeMo/Megatron rewrite.
fp8_monkey_patch_broken.verdict: FP8 retrofit on current stack is dead end. Stay with bf16.

cuda_visible_devices_empty_string.cause: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES set to empty string '' (not unset) — CUDA interprets empty as 'zero devices'. However this was MISDIAGNOSED as root cause; setting to '0' still failed. Actual root cause: container CUDA toolkit 11.8 + host driver 580.65.06 runtime mismatch or stale container state.
cuda_visible_devices_empty_string.fix: Pod restart required. CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='' is a contributing factor but not sole cause when driver version is newer than container toolkit expects.
cuda_visible_devices_empty_string.diagnosis_lesson: NEVER declare 'root cause found' until fix is verified by actual test. Hypothesis → test → confirm → declare.
cuda_visible_devices_empty_string.affected: hexa-c4 pod (u01lnnu8ywt92p) RTX 3090, CUDA 11.8 container, driver 580.65.06
cuda_visible_devices_empty_string.severity: MEDIUM — blocks GPU use on existing pod

graphql_api_schema_drift.symptom: GraphQL validation error: 'Field ports must not have a selection since type String has no subfields'
graphql_api_schema_drift.cause: RunPod GraphQL schema changed — 'ports' field is String (not object) in pod query, but object with subfields in runtime.ports
graphql_api_schema_drift.fix: Use runtime{ports{ip isIpPublic privatePort publicPort type}} for SSH info. Top-level 'ports' is just '22/tcp' string.
graphql_api_schema_drift.wrong_query: query{myself{pods{ports{ip publicPort privatePort}}}} — ports at pod level is String
graphql_api_schema_drift.severity: LOW — wastes API calls but recoverable

h100_global_stockout.symptom: Pod stuck in 'RUNNING' with runtime=NO (provisioning) indefinitely
h100_global_stockout.cause: H100 SXM and A100 80GB both out of stock on RunPod secure+community cloud (2026-04-16 evening KST)
h100_global_stockout.fix: Wait in queue (auto-allocates when available). Alternatives: Lambda Labs, Vast.ai, or use existing lower-tier pod for smoke tests.
h100_global_stockout.lesson: Always have a fallback plan. Check GPU availability BEFORE stopping existing pods.

volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.symptom: Container restart silently wipes /workspace — all in-progress artifacts (binary, corpus, checkpoints, logs) vanish with zero warning
volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.cause: Pod created with volumeInGb=0 (no persistent network volume). /workspace is container-local MooseFS scratch that is recreated on any container restart (OOM, host reschedule, manual restart).
volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.evidence: shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json — pod u744u0lbts5v1w anima-clm1b-r4-nvl, SSH port rotated 16833→14248 at 13:50 UTC, post-restart /workspace EMPTY (no train_clm_native binary, no corpus_clm_r4.txt, no ckpt_clm1b_r4/). ckpt step_5000.hexackpt (3.51GB) was being written when restart hit and is unrecoverable.
volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.fix: Choose one: (a) attach persistent network volume (--volumeSize >=50, mount to /workspace); OR (b) mandate periodic rclone sync to R2 every save_every steps (NOT post-training only) so at worst one checkpoint-interval of work is lost. Default policy for CLM/ALM training going forward: R2 periodic sync mandatory when volumeInGb=0.
volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.incident_cost_usd: ~1.0 (20 min @ $3.07/hr H100 NVL) plus 20 min training loss
volumeless_ephemeral_wipe.severity: CRITICAL — guaranteed total data loss on any container restart

silent_cpu_fallback.symptom: Training binary runs with 98.9% single-core CPU, 229GB RSS, 0% GPU utilization, 91W GPU power (idle draw) for 20+ minutes with no error despite GPU allocation
silent_cpu_fallback.cause: Native train_clm binary was built via CPU path (gcc -lm without -lcudart/-lcublas) OR CUDA kernel dispatch missing from hot loop. No runtime check for GPU kernel launches, so single-core CPU execution looks like 'training' to the orchestrator.
silent_cpu_fallback.evidence: shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json — pre-restart snapshot shows pid 479 at 98.9% CPU, 218.5GB RSS, power_w=91 (H100 NVL idle draw), gpu_util_pct=0, vram_used_mb=40614 (leaked allocation, no kernel launches, not listed in nvidia-smi processes). Compare: GPU training binary must show >10% GPU util within 60s of launch.
silent_cpu_fallback.fix: Halt-and-report preflight: (1) ldd <binary> | grep -E 'libcudart|libcublas|libcudnn' — if absent, FAIL; (2) within 60s of launch, sample nvidia-smi dmon — if gpu_util_pct <5% AND power_w <150W, KILL process and raise 'CPU-only binary detected'. training/native/build.hexa has explicit CPU fallback path (build_cpu → gcc -lm only) that must not be silently selected for GPU training rounds.
silent_cpu_fallback.applied_to_fn: training/deploy/clm_r4_launch.hexa::main (launch orchestrator), training/native/build.hexa::main (build-time CPU fallback gate)
related_c_files: training/deploy/train_clm_composed.c (not .hexa — composed C source for train_clm_native; verify CUDA kernel dispatch present before linking)
silent_cpu_fallback.severity: CRITICAL — silent ~$74/day burn on idle GPU while appearing to train

stdout_head_null_route.symptom: Training command piped to `| head -30` drops all output after line 30 with zero log file written. Debugging impossible post-mortem: no error message, no stack trace, no progress.
stdout_head_null_route.cause: Pipe termination pattern `./binary 2>&1 | head -30` closes the read end after 30 lines. Binary either (a) receives SIGPIPE on next write and tolerates it silently, or (b) OS buffers subsequent writes into the void. Distinct from stdout_buffering (which is about line buffering/tee reception) — this is intentional-but-harmful pipe termination that looks like a log command but is a data-destroyer.
stdout_head_null_route.evidence: shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json — parent_cmd was `./train_clm_native 2>&1 | head -30`, parent_stdout_sink='head -30 — stdout after first 30 lines was silently dropped (SIGPIPE tolerated by binary)'. No log file existed anywhere; metrics entirely unrecoverable.
stdout_head_null_route.fix: MANDATORY pattern: `<binary> 2>&1 | tee /workspace/<run>.log` (always tee to file). Forbidden patterns: `| head`, `| tail -f` without `tee` upstream, `>/dev/null`, `&> /dev/null`, any pipe that discards after N lines. If truncation needed for live viewing, use `tee log | head -30` not `tee /dev/null | head -30`.
stdout_head_null_route.applied_to_fn: training/deploy/clm_r4_launch.hexa::main (runner emits tee pipeline — verify no downstream head/tail truncation)
stdout_head_null_route.severity: HIGH — no log = no debug = re-burn training cost to reproduce

ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.symptom: SSH connection to active pod succeeds initially, then 'Connection refused' or 'No route to host' partway through a session despite pod desired_status=RUNNING
ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.cause: RunPod SSH port rotates whenever container restarts (OOM, host reschedule, manual restart). Existing SSH sessions survive the TCP connection but new connections to the old port fail. runpodctl pod list reflects the new port but the stale value cached in tmux/env does not.
ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.evidence: shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json — pod u744u0lbts5v1w ssh_port_initial=16833 rotated to ssh_port_post_restart=14248 at ~13:50 UTC during an active health check session. IP remained 216.81.245.55.
ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.fix: After any suspected restart (pod_uptime reset, /workspace wiped, tmux session gone), ALWAYS re-fetch port via `runpodctl pod list | grep <id>` or `runpodctl ssh info <id>` before re-connecting. Do not rely on cached SSH port from session start. Detection heuristic: if `ssh` hangs or returns 'refused' on a pod that was working, first step is port re-fetch, not network diagnosis.
ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.applied_to_fn: No dedicated clm_health_check.hexa exists yet in training/deploy/ — health check was performed one-shot via direct SSH + runpodctl and results committed to shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json. Inventory-only entry until a health-check .hexa is authored.
ssh_port_rotation_mid_session.severity: MEDIUM — recoverable but causes 10-30 min of confused debugging

ckpt_midwrite_corruption.symptom: Checkpoint file exists with plausible size but is truncated/corrupt because the writer was killed mid-write. Distinct from checkpoint_corruption_zero_byte (0-byte case) — here the file is non-zero and may even be 60-80% complete, making the corruption much harder to detect than a 0-byte tripwire.
ckpt_midwrite_corruption.cause: Training process writes large checkpoint (multi-GB) directly to the final path. Restart/kill/OOM during write leaves the destination file partially populated. Without atomic rename (.tmp → fsync → rename), there is no torn-write protection.
ckpt_midwrite_corruption.evidence: shared/state/clm_r4_health_20260416.json — /workspace/ckpt_clm1b_r4/step_5000.hexackpt grew from 3.32GB → 3.51GB over ~60s of health checks (clearly still being written), then container restart hit and the whole file was ephemerally wiped before any completion signal. Even absent the wipe, the 3.51GB file would have been a torn write.
ckpt_midwrite_corruption.fix: Atomic save pattern (applies to .hexackpt/.pt/.safetensors/any large blob): (1) write to <path>.tmp, (2) fsync the .tmp file handle, (3) os.replace / rename <path>.tmp → <path> (atomic on same filesystem). On resume, sweep for *.tmp and delete. Never write directly to the final path name.
ckpt_midwrite_corruption.applied_to_fn: training/deploy/clm_r4_launch.hexa::main (wraps python trainer — must verify trainer uses atomic save; enforce at orchestrator level with post-run scan for .tmp residue)
ckpt_midwrite_corruption.severity: HIGH — silent data corruption is worse than visible failure

RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.symptom: Launcher preflight passes static + local dry-run, but pod-side training silently aborts on huggingface.from_pretrained 401 Unauthorized.
RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.cause: Local preflight may SKIP on HF_TOKEN (Mac dev has no token), but the pod-side runner must re-gate. Without a pod-side fail-closed check, training launches against empty token and hits HF Hub 401 mid-download — no meaningful error surface, tmux log shows only traceback fragments.
RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.evidence: shared/state/alm_a1_launch_ready_20260416.json#preflight_dry_run_result.checks.4_hf_token → 'FAIL: HF_TOKEN unset' on local dev; runner emitted for pod re-checks via bash '[[ -z "${HF_TOKEN:-}" ]] && exit 1'.
RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.fix: Two-stage gate: (1) local preflight reports FAIL non-blocking (dev doesn't need token); (2) emitted bash runner re-checks HF_TOKEN as FIRST gate after nvidia-smi, refuses launch with exit 1 when unset. Never treat local SKIP as pass.
RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.affected: Any ALM LoRA launcher fetching HF base weights (Qwen/Llama/Mistral).
RUNPOD_OPERATOR_HF_TOKEN_GATE.severity: HIGH — silent data loss: operator thinks training started but nothing happens.

RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.symptom: Pod terminates (stockout, preemption, manual stop) before end-of-training rclone block runs → 0 artifacts on R2 despite step_N reached.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.cause: Deferring ALL rclone to post-training single-batch upload (per mfs_quota rule) means pod death between last in-memory ckpt and the rclone block leaves R2 empty. A 48-hour run can accumulate 10+ intermediate ckpts that never leave MooseFS.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.evidence: Pod tubq10zkct0t8f (ALM-P4-4 32B 2-GPU) terminated before writing step_3000 to R2 → shared/state/anima_train.json closed P4-4 as 'superseded_by_14b' per shared/state/extreme_100pct_gate_audit_20260416.json (commit 039cea64). Cost: $100-300 sunk + zero salvageable artifact.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.fix: Background rclone copy every ≤500 steps via post-save hook (not end-of-training only). scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa inspects label=production on every save_every boundary and triggers rclone WITHIN the MFS quota budget (save_every≥500 spacing + bounded rclone bandwidth ≠ synchronous in-training rclone+torch.save). Contrast with forbidden pattern: sync rclone co-located with torch.save in same step.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.affected: All long-running (>4h) ALM/CLM training, especially 2-GPU 32B+ with 48h+ wall time.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.severity: CRITICAL — silent data loss + roadmap closure as 'superseded' to hide the gap.
RUNPOD_POD_TERMINATED_WITHOUT_R2_UPLOAD.applied_to_fn: training/train_alm_14b.hexa::main (python runner save-every branch invokes invoke_ckpt_backup_hook); scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa::main + build_rclone_cmd

RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.symptom: Roadmap JSON wall_progress_pct shows 92% but auto_sync recomputes 50% — discrepancy traces to a pod-gated task marked 'in_progress (per roadmap)' while the pod itself is already terminated.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.cause: Roadmap tasks tied to pod artifacts (ALM-P4-4 32B step_3000) assume pod still running. When pod terminates without completing, the task status doesn't automatically flip to 'superseded' or 'aborted' — it stays 'in_progress', inflating wall_progress.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.evidence: shared/state/extreme_100pct_gate_audit_20260416.json line 250: '92% value in aggregate_state.wall_progress_pct was MANUALLY set or set by a different code path than auto_sync (which consistently computes 50%)'. Pod tubq10zkct0t8f terminated but ALM-P4-4 stayed marked as in-progress until explicit 2026-04-16T14:00Z closure.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.fix: Before claiming any pod-gated status as active, run `runpodctl pod list` freshness check. If pod not in live list, task must be auto-flipped to status='superseded' or 'aborted' with pod_id in the rationale. Roadmap sync loop should cross-check pod IDs against live inventory.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.affected: Roadmap sync + extreme_sync.hexa + any gate audit consuming roadmap JSON.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.severity: HIGH — misleads goal-tracking, hides abandonment as progress.
RUNPOD_SUPERSEDED_POD_INFLATES_ROADMAP.reason: no ALM-owned enforcement point — see sibling agent roadmap domain (harness / extreme_sync). Left as inventory-only cross-reference.

RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.symptom: Silent R2 bloat (experiment ckpts land in production prefix) OR silent data loss (production ckpts skipped as experiment).
RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.cause: ckpt_label is a string field in config + sidecar. Per shared/config/ckpt_policy.json, 'production' → R2 retain, 'experiment' → R2 discard, 'archive' → R2 retain under archive/ prefix. Without strict string-match gating, a typo or missing label defaults to unintended routing.
RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.fix: Hard-coded mirror of ckpt_policy labels in scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa::label_uploads (avoids jq dependency on pod): production/archive → true, experiment → false, unknown label → warn + false (fail closed). Trainer writes sidecar ckpt_meta.json with 'label' field; launcher validates cfg_label == 'production' before proceeding. Linter scripts/ckpt_name_lint.hexa cross-checks basename against naming_convention.{alm,clm}.regex.
RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.affected: scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa; training/train_alm_14b.hexa (invoke_ckpt_backup_hook + --ckpt-label); training/launch_alm_a1.hexa (ckpt_label production invariant); scripts/ckpt_name_lint.hexa (naming convention cross-check).
RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.severity: HIGH — cost/correctness: bloated R2 costs money, missing R2 ckpt loses training.
RUNPOD_CKPT_LABEL_VS_R2_POLICY.applied_to_fn: training/launch_alm_a1.hexa::main (cfg_label production guard); training/train_alm_14b.hexa::main (python runner --ckpt-label threaded to hook); scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa::label_uploads + r2_prefix_for; scripts/ckpt_name_lint.hexa::lint_basename

RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.symptom: Local smoke test reports 33/33 PASS, operator assumes pod will launch, but pod-side runner refuses due to HF_TOKEN unset / compute_cap < 8.0 / disk < 100GB.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.cause: Smoke test and launch readiness are distinct gates. Smoke covers static config parse + dry-run emission + JSON shape (33 asserts on Mac dev). Pod-side gates (HF_TOKEN, GPU bf16 cap, disk) are re-checked by the emitted bash runner at launch time and CANNOT be satisfied from local dev.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.evidence: shared/state/alm_a1_launch_ready_20260416.json separates smoke_test_result (33/33 on Mac) from preflight_dry_run_result (HF_TOKEN FAIL on Mac, expected) from operator_responsibilities_on_pod (4 explicit pod-side checks). ready_for_launch=true despite HF_TOKEN local-FAIL because that blocker is pod-operator-owned, not a code defect.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.fix: Two-tier reporting: smoke test asserts code invariants (dry-run emission, JSON shape); preflight separately reports pod-specific gates (HF_TOKEN, GPU, disk, R2 prefix) with explicit LOCAL/POD label. operator_responsibilities_on_pod list is part of launch_ready artifact. Never claim 'ready_for_launch' purely from smoke pass.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.affected: Any ALM/CLM launcher with remote pod target; roadmap gates predicated on 'smoke PASS' alone.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.severity: MEDIUM — operator-facing gap causes failed launches + time waste.
RUNPOD_SMOKE_PASS_VS_LAUNCH_READY_GAP.applied_to_fn: training/launch_alm_a1.hexa::main (emits runner with all pod-side re-checks); training/alm_a1_preflight.hexa::main (returns all_ok + issues list distinguishing LOCAL SKIP from POD FAIL)

pod_deletion_policy.rule: Delete pod IMMEDIATELY after training completes. Zero idle tolerance.
pod_deletion_policy.sequence: 1. Training complete -> 2. Download/verify checkpoints -> 3. rclone to R2 -> 4. runpodctl remove pod <id>
pod_deletion_policy.special_case_72b: Pod 2 (2xH100 $5.38/hr) for 72B training: delete IMMEDIATELY after checkpoint download+R2 upload. $5.38/hr idle is unacceptable.
pod_deletion_policy.idle_monitoring: If pod is idle (no tmux session, no GPU utilization), investigate and delete within 5 minutes.

cloud_comparison.note: RunPod is secondary provider. Vast.ai is 30-50% cheaper for raw GPU time.
cloud_comparison.runpod_advantage: Better CLI tooling (runpodctl), serverless inference, stable SSH
cloud_comparison.vast_ai_advantage: 30-50% cheaper GPU hours, better GPU availability

tmux_session_management.critical_rule: Always use 'python -u' for unbuffered stdout. Without it, tee receives nothing.

standard_pod_setup_recipe.step_3: apt-get update -qq && DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y -qq build-essential clang lld git curl ca-certificates libblis-dev
standard_pod_setup_recipe.step_6: pip install peft==0.13.2 transformers==4.45.2 (if ALM LoRA)
standard_pod_setup_recipe.step_7: dd if=/dev/zero of=/workspace/_q_test bs=1M count=20000 status=none && rm /workspace/_q_test (MFS quota preflight)
standard_pod_setup_recipe.step_9: Launch training in tmux with python -u
standard_pod_setup_recipe.step_10: After training: rclone to R2, verify, then runpodctl remove pod <id>
